title: Dolphin Watching Catamaran Hire in Marbella: 2026 Costs
html:
Dolphin watching catamaran hire in Marbella sets off from Puerto Banus and the Marbella marina, gliding into the Alboran Sea where bottlenose and striped dolphins are common. Expect a day price band of roughly EUR 350 to EUR 1,800 on BoatHire24, depending on the boat and crew.

Where to set off in Marbella

Marbella sits on the western Costa del Sol, and most charters leave from a cluster of harbours near the old town, each opening onto sheltered water where dolphin pods feed through the warmer months.

  • Puerto Jose Banus: the glamorous Puerto Banus marina, the busiest departure point for catamarans.
  • Puerto Deportivo de Marbella: the central marina beside the old town.
  • Puerto de la Bajadilla: the local fishing and leisure port.
  • Cabopino: a pretty marina near the dunes, towards Fuengirola.
  • Estepona: a neighbouring port roughly 15 nautical miles west.

Which boat to rent in Marbella for dolphin watching?

A stable sailing catamaran or a roomy motor yacht suits dolphin watching best, because both offer a steady platform and shaded deck space for spotting. The options below run from budget to crewed luxury.

  1. Sailing catamaran: ideal for families wanting shade and space, up to 11 guests, from EUR 600 per day.
  2. RIB or speedboat: quick to reach feeding pods, best for 6 to 8 guests, from EUR 350 a half day.
  3. Sport motor yacht: a fast cruiser such as the Sunseeker Predator 72, up to 12 guests, from EUR 1,500 per day; see the layout on the Sunseeker Predator 72 charter page.
  4. Flybridge yacht: a raised helm and sun deck for relaxed watching, up to 12 guests, from EUR 1,200 per day.
  5. Day sailer: a classic monohull for a quieter, wind-driven outing, around 8 guests, from EUR 450 per day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Marbella?

Marbella boat prices in 2026 run from about EUR 45 per seat on a shared trip to well over EUR 1,800 for a crewed yacht for the day. The bands below show what each style costs.

  • A shared dolphin-watching tour costs roughly EUR 45 to EUR 70 per person.
  • A small private motorboat for a half day starts at EUR 350.
  • A catamaran for a full day typically runs EUR 600 to EUR 1,200.
  • A crewed luxury yacht for the day reaches EUR 1,800 or more.

Prices climb in midsummer, so booking early secures the better rates, and you can scan day charters in Marbella to compare your dates.

When is the best season to see dolphins off Marbella?

The high season runs from June to September, when warm, settled water and long days give the most reliable dolphin sightings. Spring and autumn are quieter and calmer at dawn. The dominant local wind is the Levante, an easterly that can raise a short chop, while the gentler Poniente blows from the west.

Book a morning slot between June and September: the sea off Marbella is usually glassy before the Levante wind freshens after midday, giving the calmest water and the clearest dolphin sightings of the day.

Spain requires a recognised licence to skipper larger or faster boats yourself, while many smaller craft can be hired without one; the operator confirms the exact requirements for each boat before you sail.

How does booking a catamaran in Marbella work?

You choose a boat and date online, send a request to the operator, then confirm with a deposit once availability is agreed. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Marbella or list your own.

Most outings last two to four hours, though full-day charters of seven to eight hours suit larger groups. A typical run reaches productive water within three nautical miles of the marina.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 45 per seat to EUR 1,800 per day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: 2 to 4 hours for dolphin watching, up to 8 hours full day
  • Capacity: 6 to 12 guests on most charters
  • Currency: EUR
  • Main departure: Puerto Banus, Marbella

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

Are you guaranteed to see dolphins on a Marbella trip?

Sightings are common but never guaranteed, as dolphins are wild animals. Pods of bottlenose and striped dolphins appear on most warm-season outings from Puerto Banus, as operators time trips to known feeding grounds.

How many people fit on a dolphin-watching catamaran?

Most catamarans in Marbella carry 10 to 12 guests comfortably for a day trip, with shaded seating and deck space at the bow. Smaller motorboats suit groups of 6 to 8.

Is dolphin watching suitable for young children?

Yes, calm catamarans are a popular family choice because the wide, stable deck keeps movement gentle. Operators provide child life jackets, and a morning departure usually means flatter water for young children.

How far in advance should you book?

Booking two to three weeks ahead is wise in the June to September peak, when the best boats and morning slots fill quickly, though outside high season a charter can often be secured within days.

A morning on the Alboran Sea, watching dolphins arc beside a steady catamaran, is one of the Costa del Sol's simplest pleasures, and easily arranged for 2026. If you own a suitable boat in Marbella, you can also become a host and earn from quiet weekdays through the BoatHire24 marketplace.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
